findings.description: Stored XSS (CWE-79, OWASP A05): user-controlled display_name is interpolated into HTML <td> with no escaping, so a malicious display name executes in the admin's authenticated session when the report is rendered, enabling admin account takeover.
Stored XSS (CWE-79): the event 'action' value is interpolated into HTML output unescaped on the same sink, providing a second injection vector if action strings are attacker-influenced.
Reflected XSS (CWE-79): the public 'title' parameter flows unescaped through PAGE_TEMPLATE.format into both <title> and <h1>; any request-derived title injects arbitrary HTML/JS.

Path traversal / arbitrary file write (CWE-22, OWASP A01): save_report opens an unvalidated out_path in write mode, allowing attacker-influenced paths (e.g. '../../') to overwrite arbitrary files if out_path derives from request input.

XSS sink (CWE-79): created_at is also interpolated into HTML unescaped; lower attacker influence as it is a DB-generated timestamp, but still an unescaped output sink that should be escaped uniformly.

Missing output-encoding strategy / no CSP (OWASP A02 defense-in-depth): the module emits HTML via str.format with no markupsafe/html.escape/autoescaping and sets no Content-Security-Policy; the CSP portion belongs to the HTTP layer (out of file scope) but the absent encoding is the root enabler of the XSS findings.

Unbounded result set (soft availability/DoS, OWASP A06): fetch_events loads all matching rows into memory with no pagination, so a large date range could exhaust memory; primarily a performance concern, deferred to the performance critic.
